issues.title: Unified domain model and intake contract migration
issues.description: Finish consolidating the canonical Go task, intake, and workflow vocabulary so downstream runtime slices stop inventing parallel compatibility contracts.

comments.body:
Progress update: restored canonical Go task-contract parity for the remaining `models.py` identity/budget-override gap. `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task.go` now accepts legacy `task_id` JSON input while preserving the canonical `id` field, emits both identifiers on JSON output for migration compatibility, and carries the Python budget-override fields (`budget_override_actor`, `budget_override_reason`, `budget_override_amount`) on the canonical Go task type. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/domain/task_test.go` for JSON round-trip behavior and in `bigclaw-go/internal/api/server_test.go` to verify `/tasks` accepts legacy `task_id` payloads.

Completed the remaining Go-side audit semantics parity for policy and approval events in `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/*`.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/audit.go` now validates spec-backed execution audit events before JSONL persistence, using top-level `task_id` / `run_id` plus payload fields so malformed approval, takeover, budget-override, and handoff events cannot be written.
- `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/recorder.go` now exposes `RecordSpecEvent`, a spec-aware recorder path that rejects malformed audit events before they mutate in-memory recorder state.
- Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/audit_test.go` and `bigclaw-go/internal/observability/recorder_test.go` for top-level identifier handling, missing required fields, sink rejection, and recorder rejection/acceptance behavior.

issues.title: Workflow orchestration and scheduler loop migration
issues.description: Close the Go execution loop with workflow-aware orchestration, journal state, and runtime closeout so active development no longer needs the Python workflow engine.

comments.body:
Improved the Go orchestrator loop in `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop.go` so it no longer waits a full poll interval before touching queued work, drains available work within a single cycle, and falls back to a safe default poll interval when unset. Added focused coverage in `bigclaw-go/internal/orchestrator/loop_test.go` for immediate startup, per-cycle draining, and default poll interval behavior.

Hardened the Go worker runtime defaults for the active orchestration slice.

What changed:
- `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime.go` now falls back to sane default lease and task timeout values when `LeaseTTL` or `TaskTimeout` are unset, preventing zero-value heartbeat ticker panics and immediate timeout cancellations in unattended bootstrap/runtime paths.
- The runtime now uses those effective defaults consistently for `LeaseNext`, heartbeat renewals, cancellation polling, and execution deadlines.
- Added `TestRuntimeUsesDefaultTimeoutAndLeaseWhenUnset` in `bigclaw-go/internal/worker/runtime_test.go` to lock the zero-value runtime behavior.
